content:
```json
{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "New queue-based consumer implementation has thread safety and offset tracking logic that requires careful validation, plus potential unbounded queue memory growth.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,
    "total": 0,
    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Thread Safety - Race Condition",
      "file": "src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "Unsynchronized access to partition_locks dictionary",
      "description": "In `_get_partition_lock()`, the check `if lock:` followed by `setdefault()` creates a race condition. Between checking and setting, another thread could create the same lock, causing multiple Lock objects for one partition. This breaks the per-partition locking invariant and can lead to concurrent modifications to partition state without proper synchronization.",
      "suggestion": "Use a module-level lock or Lock() in __init__ to synchronize partition_locks dictionary access, or use a thread-safe dictionary implementation: `with self._dict_lock: return self.partition_locks.setdefault(partition, threading.Lock())`"
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Memory Leak - Unbounded Queue Growth",
      "file": "src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py",
      "line": 178,
      "title": "Queue without max size limit can cause unbounded memory growth",
      "description": "The queue.Queue() is instantiated without a maxsize parameter. If the result processor is slow or blocked, items will accumulate unboundedly in memory, potentially causing OOM. This is especially problematic in a consumer that receives messages from Kafka.",
      "suggestion": "Add a maxsize parameter to queue.Queue() initialization based on expected throughput and available memory: `queue.Queue(maxsize=1000)` or similar configurable limit"
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Offset Tracking - Logical Correctness",
      "file": "src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py",
      "line": 93,
      "title": "Offset cleanup logic may lose offset state prematurely",
      "description": "In `mark_committed()`, all offsets <= committed offset are removed from `all_offsets`. However, if `get_committable_offsets()` is called between when an offset is committed and when it's cleaned up, the logic in line 77 (`min_offset = min(all_offsets)`) could change, causing gaps in offset tracking logic. The cleanup should be more conservative or coordinated with the committable offset calculation.",
      "suggestion": "Keep a separate `committed_offsets` set instead of removing from `all_offsets`, or ensure `mark_committed()` is only called after all workers have finished with those offsets"
    }
  ]
}
```
